steps.action: Raqs sharqi (Arabic: رقص شرقي, [ˈɾɑʔsˤ ˈʃæɾʔi]; with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: Raqs sharqi (Arabic: رقص شرقي, [ˈɾɑʔsˤ ˈʃæɾʔi]; with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: This has come to be considered the classical sty
steps.text: This has come to be considered the classical style of dance in Egypt by the 1950s.

steps.action: These dancers were famous not only for their rol
steps.text: These dancers were famous not only for their role in Egyptian films, but also for their performances at the "Opera Casino" opened in 1925 by Badia Masabni.

steps.action: This venue was a popular place for influential m
steps.text: This venue was a popular place for influential musicians and choreographers from both the US and Europe, so many of the developments pioneered here can be considered new developments in the dance.

steps.action: Later dancers who were influenced by these artis
steps.text: Later dancers who were influenced by these artists are Soheir Zaki, Nagwa Fouad, Fifi Abdou, and Dalilah.

steps.action: Though the basic movements of raqs sharqi are un
steps.text: Though the basic movements of raqs sharqi are unchanged, the dance form continues to evolve.

steps.action: Nelly Mazloum and Mahmoud Reda are noted for inc
steps.text: Nelly Mazloum and Mahmoud Reda are noted for incorporating elements of ballet, and their influence can be seen in modern Egyptian dancers who stand on relevé as they turn or travel in a circle or figure eight.

steps.action: It is therefore becoming more common to wear a l
steps.text: It is therefore becoming more common to wear a long, figure-hugging lycra one-piece gown with strategically placed cut-outs filled in with sheer, flesh-coloured fabric and the skirt is sometimes embroidered.

steps.action: Egyptian dancers traditionally dance in bare fee
steps.text: Egyptian dancers traditionally dance in bare feet, but these days often wear shoes and even high heels.

steps.action: but many Egyptians continue to employ native Egy
steps.text: but many Egyptians continue to employ native Egyptian dancers for special events.

steps.action: Strict moral laws prevent a lot of local Egyptia
steps.text: Strict moral laws prevent a lot of local Egyptian dancers from performing in public spaces so many dancers performing for tourists in nightclubs today are foreigners.

steps.action: Belly dancers in Egypt have restrictions placed
steps.text: Belly dancers in Egypt have restrictions placed on their costume and movements.

steps.action: Most notably, no floor work is permitted and the
steps.text: Most notably, no floor work is permitted and the dancer's midriff must be covered.

steps.action: All rose to fame between 1960 and 1980, and are
steps.text: All rose to fame between 1960 and 1980, and are still popular today.

steps.action: A sheer body stocking covers the midsection.
steps.text: A sheer body stocking covers the midsection.

steps.action: However, many Egyptian nightclubs don't necessar
steps.text: However, many Egyptian nightclubs don't necessarily follow the government guidelines.
